Incident Quotes:
At dawn on Saturday, October 21st, 2023, an alleged Israeli airstrike hit the al-Bahtini’s family home located in front of al-Julani Mosque in the al-Tuffah neighborhood, east of Gaza City, reportedly killing at least 23 civilians spanning three generations and causing an unknown number of injuries, according to sources. One militant, 37-year-old Ahmed al-Bahtini, was also killed.

Among those killed in the strike were al-Julani Mosque’s Imam, 58-year-old Sheikh Arafat al-Bahtini Abu Muhammad and his wife, along with their children and grandchildren: 37-year-old Sheikh Ahmed al-Bahtini Abu Anas and his son, 14-year-old Jamal al-Din Ahmed al-Bahtini; Jihad al-Bahtini, his wife, their infant son Omar al-Bahtini, and Jihad’s mother, Umm Alaa, as well as Jihad’s cousins, 34-year-old Hamza Badr al-Din al-Bahtini and Yasser al-Bahtini. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0424-october-21-2023/

Incident Summary: At least 1 combatant (Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ)) was killed in this incident, along with 4 relatives and 12 neighbors.
Incident Quotes:
On Tuesday, October 24th 2023, at least 21 civilians were reportedly killed and a number of others were injured during an alleged Israeli airstrike on the two homes of the Abu Warda family, near the Al Nazla roundabout, in Jabalia Al Nazla, in the northern Gaza Strip.
...
...
Naheel Sai’d identified the names of Ziad Al-Muqayd’s wife Reham Shekhda Khader, two sons, 11-year-old Muhammad Ziyad Al-Muqayd and six-year-old Karim Ziad Al-Muqayed, and his daughter, nine-year-old Maryam Ziyad Al-Muqayd. Naheel reported that the family were killed when the house in which they had took refuge was bombed. Accompanying this post was a picture of two of the children on a ride at a funfair.

In a post on Facebook, Saheel Al-Muqayyad posted a prayer to Ziad Al-Muqayd, his beloved brother and his family, as did Husam Abu Hamze and other friends and family members. A Facebook account called Sons of Al-Jarn Neighbourhood paid tribute to Ziad and his family saying, “the people of al-Jarn neighbourhood mourn”. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0546-october-24-2023/

Naheel Sai’d identified the names of Ziad Al-Muqayd’s wife Reham Shekhda Khader, two sons, 11-year-old Muhammad Ziyad Al-Muqayd and six-year-old Karim Ziad Al-Muqayed, and his daughter, nine-year-old Maryam Ziyad Al-Muqayd. Naheel reported that the family were killed when the house in which they had took refuge was bombed. Accompanying this post was a picture of two of the children on a ride at a funfair.

In a post on Facebook, Saheel Al-Muqayyad posted a prayer to Ziad Al-Muqayd, his beloved brother and his family, as did Husam Abu Hamze and other friends and family members. A Facebook account called Sons of Al-Jarn Neighbourhood paid tribute to Ziad and his family saying, “the people of al-Jarn neighbourhood mourn”. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0546-october-24-2023/

Incident Quotes:
During the afternoon/evening of May 13, 2025, the Israeli military dropped dozens of bombs that hit and damaged the European Hospital and surrounding homes in Khan Younis, Gaza, killing at least 15 civilians, including two children and two women, and injuring 70 others, including four journalists. The Israeli military announced the strike, initially stating that they were targeting Hamas militants and a tunnel network underneath the European Hospital, and later announcing that the strike hit senior Hamas militant Mohammad Sinwar and a number of other fighters. Sinwar was likely one of at least three Hamas militants killed.

Airwars researchers were able to identify the names of 15 civilians killed in the strike, though some reports put the death toll as high as 48.

Journalist Mohamed Awad reported on Facebook that “martyrs and wounded” had resulted from an Israeli air strike on the Gaza European Hospital and the surrounding area, and included a list of people killed: 29-year-old Hamza Jamil Abdul Hadi Safi, 38-year-old Muhammad Rafiq Abdul Hadi Safi, 22-year-old Jamil Muhammad Jamil Safi, 28-year-old Abdullah Jamal Suleiman al-Farra, 36-year-old Ahmad Faris Jabr Radi, 58-year-old Muhammad Mahmoud Abdul Karim al-Afghani (Abu Iyad), his son 28-year-old Anas Muhammad Mahmoud al-Afghani, his daughter 14-year-old Malak Muhammad Mahmoud al-Afghani, and 23-year-old Bilal Bashir Abu Amuna.

On May 13, Facebook account “People of Ma’an and eastern Khan Yunis”, also published a list of those killed in the violence. The account was the first to report the deaths of 18-year-old Muhammad Saeed Muhammad al-Aqqad and 54-year-old Kifah Ibrahim Khalil al-Qudsi (al-Afghani), wife of Mohammad Mahmoud and mother of Anas and siblings. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt130525j-may-13-2025/

Journalist Mohamed Awad reported on Facebook that “martyrs and wounded” had resulted from an Israeli air strike on the Gaza European Hospital and the surrounding area, and included a list of people killed: 29-year-old Hamza Jamil Abdul Hadi Safi, 38-year-old Muhammad Rafiq Abdul Hadi Safi, 22-year-old Jamil Muhammad Jamil Safi, 28-year-old Abdullah Jamal Suleiman al-Farra, 36-year-old Ahmad Faris Jabr Radi, 58-year-old Muhammad Mahmoud Abdul Karim al-Afghani (Abu Iyad), his son 28-year-old Anas Muhammad Mahmoud al-Afghani, his daughter 14-year-old Malak Muhammad Mahmoud al-Afghani, and 23-year-old Bilal Bashir Abu Amuna.

On May 13, Facebook account “People of Ma’an and eastern Khan Yunis”, also published a list of those killed in the violence. The account was the first to report the deaths of 18-year-old Muhammad Saeed Muhammad al-Aqqad and 54-year-old Kifah Ibrahim Khalil al-Qudsi (al-Afghani), wife of Mohammad Mahmoud and mother of Anas and siblings. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt130525j-may-13-2025/

Incident Summary: At least 1 combatant commander (Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ)) was killed in this incident, along with 11 relatives.
Incident Quotes:
At approximately 1am on October 22nd 2023, 12 civilians, including at least 11 members of the Mansour family, were reportedly killed when an alleged Israeli bombardment hit their home on Aed Al Bashiti Street, in the Al Geneina neighbourhood of Rafah. An unspecified number of others were wounded. A thirteenth member of the Mansour family was also killed, and was a militant affiliated with Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ).

On March 20, 2026, PIJ published a non-exhaustive list of commanders killed in Gaza since October 7, 2023. 28-year-old Abdullah Daniel Mansour was among those named and photographed. As such, he has been recorded as a militant.

53-year-old Nimat Muhammad Mansour (Bawadi, wife of Ghaleb Kamel Mansour) was reportedly killed in the attack, alongside her two children 25-year-old Mahmoud Ghaleb Mansour and 17-year-old Fatima Ghaleb Mansour. Nimat’s sister, 47-year-old Khitam Muhammad Mansour Bawadi was also killed in the attack, along with four of her children, 15-year-old Alaa Daniel Mansour, 20-year-old Samer Daniel Kamel Mansour, 25-year-old Bisan Daniel Mansour and 28-year-old Abdullah Daniel Mansour. Abdullah’s wife, 28-year-old Doaa Moeen Bawadi, died in the attack as did their two young daughters 3-year-old Jordi Abdullah Mansour and 6-year-old Dania Abdullah Mansour, and their son, 1-year-old Daniel Abdullah Mansour. A girl named Maria Khaled Zakaria Al-Shanti who was less than a year old was also killed. Sources reported that Doaa was pregnant at the time she was killed. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0485-october-22-2023/

Facebook: "With hearts filled with faith in God's decree and destiny, and overflowing with both sorrow and pride, we announce the martyrdom of our mujahid: the hero, Mahmoud Abu Nasr Mahmoud Nasr Khamis Bazazo.

On the evening of Thursday, June 12th, he ascended to the highest ranks of martyrdom, God willing, after a life brimming with sacrifice, during which he exemplified the most magnificent meanings of heroism and steadfastness in confronting the enemies of God, offering his pure soul as a sacrifice for his faith and homeland.

The martyr was an exemplar of sincerity and selflessness. He never retreated from the fields of jihad and was always at the forefront of the arenas of honor and dignity. We believe him to be so, and we do not presume to judge anyone before God."

Incident Quotes:
We say only what pleases God: "To God we belong, and to Him we shall return." And God willing, Paradise will be our meeting place.

Martyr Ramzi Felfel, his wife, and children

Martyr Muhammad Nafid Felfel and his brothers Hatem and Alaa
Despite the pain that grips our hearts at your loss, your martyrdom will remain a source of pride and a crown we will wear on our heads for life.
May God have mercy on you all, O Lord of the Worlds. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14ekLot94Bh/
{And give good tidings to those who patiently persevere, those who, when afflicted with calamity, say, "Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him we will return." Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy. And it is those who are the [rightly] guided.}

The eye sheds tears, and the heart grieves,

and we are truly saddened by the loss of our loved ones.

But we say only what pleases Allah.

And we see them in the words of our Lord: "And those who feared their Lord will be led to Paradise in groups."
With deepest sorrow and grief, we mourn and offer our condolences on the martyrdom of our cousins, Nafiz Abdul-Hadi Falafel (Hatem, Muhammad, and Alaa) 💔 and our cousin Ramzi Abdul God is great. And his sons and daughters. ((Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him we will return.)) O Allah, reward us for our loss and replace it with something better.

We ask Allah to have mercy on them, forgive them, and grant their families patience and solace, O Lord of the Worlds. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/18fYxRL6JZ/
The fourth moon in our family
Martyr Ramzi Abdullah Felfel "Obaid"
and his wife and children, 💔
There is no power nor strength except with God
And give good tidings to those who patiently persevere, who, when afflicted with calamity, say, "Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him we will return."

On October 31, 2023, time stands still. You have broken our hearts with your departure. Life now has no meaning; it is merely a countdown of time.
In the Jabalia massacre, my uncle, my companion, my beloved, my support, and my friend, "Abu Abdullah" Ramzi Felfel, his wife "Umm Abdullah," his son Abdullah, his son Muhammad, and his daughters Mai, Nuha, and Nada were martyred. Also martyred were the dearest people to my heart, my confidants and companions in a life that has become meaningless without them: my cousins, Muhammad Felfel, Alaa Felfel, and Hatem Felfel. I pray for the speedy recovery of my dear aunt "Umm Diaa," her daughter Umm Tariq, and my cousin Alaa. May God grant you patience, Umm. Tareq — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14WAqvvyCzB/

With hearts believing in God's decree and destiny, I bring you the news of the martyrdom of my loved ones: my dear mother, may God have mercy on her, and I ask God to grant her Paradise; 
Umm Naseem Al-Amoudi; 
my brother Muhammad Al-Amoudi (Al-Mulla); 
my brother Waseem Al-Amoudi and his wife Aya and their children Jihad, Rawad, and Alma; 
and my nephew Ahmed Kinan. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1SeYraJzNz/
In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

"And never think of those who have been killed in the cause of God as dead. Rather, they are alive with their Lord, receiving provision."
I extend my deepest condolences to my uncle Jihad Al-Amoudi (Abu Naseem) and to all my dear uncles and their children following the bombing of their home in the Jabalia refugee camp, north of Gaza, in the Al-Fakhoura area, in a cowardly assassination operation. 3- Jihad Waseem Al-Amoudi
4- Umm Naseem Al-Amoudi
5- Muhammad Jihad Al-Amoudi
6- The child Rawad Waseem Al-Amoudi
7- The child Al-Mai Waseem Al-Amoudi
8- The child Kinan Ahmed Al-Amoudi. May God have mercy on them. Be patient with God's will, for it is for the best. May God grant you a great reward and ease your suffering. We say only what pleases God: "Indeed, we belong to God, and indeed, to Him we will return." — https://www.facebook.com/sadoon32/posts/pfbid0WT3ZUh1yiyV5XjHkhpUjZJrBgZJsQDsque4nJfd4U4x6gRFydRzAY1RxWYauPqVWl

Incident Quotes:
Oh, the pain in my heart…

My beloved brother Khaled “Abu Amr”… God chose you as a martyr, you and your family, after the occupation bombed the house with everyone inside it.

My brother, dutiful to his parents and of good character… your family, loved ones, and everyone who knew you testify to that.
Your students and colleagues at Palestine School and elsewhere testify to you, for you were a virtuous educator.

Mother, your son, the beloved of your heart, has come to you.

My heart will cry for you before my eyes, for as long as I live.

My beloved nephew Amr “Abu Khaled” —
You left and left me with a wound that will not heal, you pious sheikh, mosque imam and Quran teacher…
Your loved ones and your university testify to you, for you were first in the Sharia Department at the Islamic University.
I was waiting for the day you would defend your master’s thesis, and I would be with you, proud of you.

Only days separated us from Tarawih prayers, when the mosque would have been waiting for Imam Amr Khaled Hammad.

You left and took with you your dear wife Raghad, the daughter of my beloved brother Yasser — may God grant them patience — and you took your children: little Khaled, and your beautiful daughter with blue eyes.

You were always accompanying your parents in this passing world, so you accompanied them in the afterlife, as you wished.

Tariq, my beloved, the one with the beautiful smile — a groom in Paradise.

How will the days and years pass without seeing you — if we have life remaining — for you were dutiful, like your father?

How will Ramadan come while I am waiting for you?

My dear, tender brother’s wife.
My brother’s daughters, beloved by their aunt.

Salsabil — your students at Al-Zahraa School cry for you.
Little Nour.
Israa and her children Omar and Tala.

O God, reward us in our calamity and replace it for us with something better.
O Lord, give me patience to bear this pain and loss.
God is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14fxb1hfStk/

Incident Quotes:
Oh, the pain in my heart…

My beloved brother Khaled “Abu Amr”… God chose you as a martyr, you and your family, after the occupation bombed the house with everyone inside it.

My brother, dutiful to his parents and of good character… your family, loved ones, and everyone who knew you testify to that.
Your students and colleagues at Palestine School and elsewhere testify to you, for you were a virtuous educator.

Mother, your son, the beloved of your heart, has come to you.

My heart will cry for you before my eyes, for as long as I live.

My beloved nephew Amr “Abu Khaled” —
You left and left me with a wound that will not heal, you pious sheikh, mosque imam and Quran teacher…
Your loved ones and your university testify to you, for you were first in the Sharia Department at the Islamic University.
I was waiting for the day you would defend your master’s thesis, and I would be with you, proud of you.

Only days separated us from Tarawih prayers, when the mosque would have been waiting for Imam Amr Khaled Hammad.

You left and took with you your dear wife Raghad, the daughter of my beloved brother Yasser — may God grant them patience — and you took your children: little Khaled, and your beautiful daughter with blue eyes.

You were always accompanying your parents in this passing world, so you accompanied them in the afterlife, as you wished.

Tariq, my beloved, the one with the beautiful smile — a groom in Paradise.

How will the days and years pass without seeing you — if we have life remaining — for you were dutiful, like your father?

How will Ramadan come while I am waiting for you?

My dear, tender brother’s wife.
My brother’s daughters, beloved by their aunt.

Salsabil — your students at Al-Zahraa School cry for you.
Little Nour.
Israa and her children Omar and Tala.

O God, reward us in our calamity and replace it for us with something better.
O Lord, give me patience to bear this pain and loss.
God is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14fxb1hfStk/

Incident Quotes:
Oh, the pain in my heart…

My beloved brother Khaled “Abu Amr”… God chose you as a martyr, you and your family, after the occupation bombed the house with everyone inside it.

My brother, dutiful to his parents and of good character… your family, loved ones, and everyone who knew you testify to that.
Your students and colleagues at Palestine School and elsewhere testify to you, for you were a virtuous educator.

Mother, your son, the beloved of your heart, has come to you.

My heart will cry for you before my eyes, for as long as I live.

My beloved nephew Amr “Abu Khaled” —
You left and left me with a wound that will not heal, you pious sheikh, mosque imam and Quran teacher…
Your loved ones and your university testify to you, for you were first in the Sharia Department at the Islamic University.
I was waiting for the day you would defend your master’s thesis, and I would be with you, proud of you.

Only days separated us from Tarawih prayers, when the mosque would have been waiting for Imam Amr Khaled Hammad.

You left and took with you your dear wife Raghad, the daughter of my beloved brother Yasser — may God grant them patience — and you took your children: little Khaled, and your beautiful daughter with blue eyes.

You were always accompanying your parents in this passing world, so you accompanied them in the afterlife, as you wished.

Tariq, my beloved, the one with the beautiful smile — a groom in Paradise.

How will the days and years pass without seeing you — if we have life remaining — for you were dutiful, like your father?

How will Ramadan come while I am waiting for you?

My dear, tender brother’s wife.
My brother’s daughters, beloved by their aunt.

Salsabil — your students at Al-Zahraa School cry for you.
Little Nour.
Israa and her children Omar and Tala.

O God, reward us in our calamity and replace it for us with something better.
O Lord, give me patience to bear this pain and loss.
God is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14fxb1hfStk/

Transliterated Name: Manal Abd Allah Abd al-Qadir al-Zamlai
Arabic Name: منال عبد الله عبد القادر الزاملي
Incident Quotes:
On October 12th 2023, it was reported that at least nine civilians, including at least one young child, were killed and up to ten others injured after an alleged airstrike on the Al-Zimili family home, east of Rafah. Where sources identified the belligerent, all sources attributed the strikes to Israeli forces.

Manal Al-Zamili was reportedly killed alongside her three sons Nael Atef Al-Zamili, Muhammad Atef Al-Zammili, and Saleh Atef Al-Zamili, and her daughter Amal Atef Al-Zamili. Nael was reportedly trapped under the rubble of the house, while Saleh reportedly died of injuries sustained in the bombing some time after the attack. His death was announced on October 20th by Safa.ps.

Manal’s husband was said to have been killed in the 2014 war after a bomb hit the ambulance he was driving.

Manal’s daughter Amal was killed alongside her young son (name unknown). Suhaila Al-Zamili (Al-Najjar) was also reportedly killed, the relationship to the family is unknown. The brothers Wael Nasser (Sheikh Eid) and Waseem Nasser (Sheikh Eid) were also killed in the alleged airstrike. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0173-october-12-2023/

Incident Quotes:
On the evening of October 9th, 2023, up to 15 members of the al-Madhoun family, including two women and eleven children, were killed as a result of an alleged Israeli airstrike on the al-Madhoun family home located behind the Golden Hall in the Americana area west to the town of Beit Lahyia in the northern Gaza Strip. According to the Defense for Children Palestine (DCI Palestine) report, the attack on the two-story family house happened without a prior warning.

Initially the family members and “Al-Araby Al-Jadeed” correspondent reported about 14 people killed. “The entire family was removed from the civil registry and registered with the Immortals” wrote their relative Fayeq al-Madhoun on Facebook. A family member Ahmad al-Madhoun wrote on the social media platform “X” to confirm the death of the 14 members of the al-Madhoun family, posting the picture of the rubble which used to be the family house. He wrote: “14 members of my family were martyred in a massacre and direct bombing of the Al-Madhoun family home in the Sudaniya area, including children and women!”.

Later, local news agencies Shehab Agency, Wafa News, Daqiqa News reported that the Israeli military bombed the home of the two brothers, 37-year-old Dr. Muhammad Abdel Rahim al-Madhoun and 33-year-old Dr. Ahmed Abdel Rahim al-Madhoun*, subsequently leading to their death and the death of 13 other family members of the al-Madhoun family, including Muhammad’s wife and six children and Ahmed’s wife and five children. The brothers’ cousin Abu Habib shared testimony on Facebook, posting pictures of the two brothers who lost their lives in the attack.

The following day, on October 10th, a family member Abu Mohammed Madhoun confirmed on Facebook that the six children of Muhammad Abd al-Rahim al-Madhoun killed in the airstrike were: Fatima Muhammad Abd al-Rahim al-Madhoun (female, child, 7), Amani Muhammad Abd al-Rahim al-Madhoun (female, child, 4), Mahmoud Muhammad Abd al-Rahim al-Madhoun (male, child, 9), Akram Muhammad Abdel Rahim al-Madhoun (male, child, 10), Karam Muhammad Abdel Rahim al-Madhoun (male, child 3), Watan Muhammad Abdel Rahim al-Madhoun (male, child, 1), alongside Amal Mahmoud Zayed al-Zaanin (al-Madhoun) (female, 39 years old) – likely to be Muhammad Abd al-Rahim al-Madhoun’s wife. The source added that specialised teams continued to search for the remaining members of the family under the rubble of their home. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0056-october-9-2023/

Incident Quotes:
Eleven members of the Khader family were reportedly killed and at least one other person, a woman, was injured in an alleged Israeli airstrike on their home behind Al-Najjar hospital in the Al-Geneina neighbourhood in Rafah, at dawn on October 21st 2023.

Khader family members were killed across three generations. Surviving family member Khalil Khader lost his four children: Ibrahim, aged nine; Amal, aged five; Kenan, aged two-and-a-half; and Rosa, 18 months. His 72 year-old father Ibrahim Shukri Suleiman Khader was also killed, alongside 23 year-old Mona Ibrahim Khader, 29 year-old Samar Abdullah Ali Khader (Al-Atrash) and Samar’s two daughters, Masa, aged two, and Lina, aged four. Initial reports claimed there were nine victims of the airstrike, as they did not account for the death of 33 year-old Louay Ibrahim Khader, husband to Samar and father to their children, who died on 25 October as a result of injuries sustained during the strike, as well as his 30 year-old sister Maysa Ibrahim Khader, whose body was not found until 24 November, on the roof of a neighbour’s house. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0422b-october-21-2023/

Thursday, November 23, 2023 — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1CvV7Dkyyg/
May Paradise be your abode, for I am truly speechless.

My beloved martyr, the mujahid Sami Muhammad al-Jurani; my mother, the martyr Shadia Fathi al-Jurani; my patient and steadfast sister, the martyr Rasha Sami al-Jurani; my dear sister, Dr. Haya Sami al-Jurani; my beloved martyr, Ahmad Sami al-Jurani; my sister, the martyr Nour al-Huda Sami al-Jurani; and my wife, my soul, my companion, my friend, my confidante, and the mother of my martyred children, the martyr Nida Ibrahim al-Jurani. And my martyred daughter, Dima Yahya Al-Jourani
And my martyred son, Sami Ya'in Al-Jourani
And my martyred son, Abdul Rahman Yahya Al-Jourani
And my martyred son, Muhammad Yahya Al-Jourani
And the children of my mother's uncle, the kind father's son
The child, Mahmoud Muhammad Abu Samra
Our martyred neighbor, the hero, Haitham Faisal Abu Zahri
The child, Adam Muhammad Mahmoud Abu Samra
By God, you are all martyrs, and by God, your martyrdom is a true testimony. By God, God has chosen you.
I ask God to accept you.
You have left behind a memory that history will immortalize.
By God, your loss has pained my heart.
You have left a void in my heart, my beloved ones. May God grant me patience. By God, my heart is heavy.
I have no words but, "To God we belong, and to Him we shall return."

Incident Quotes:
#Condolences #Palestine_Khan_Younis🇵🇸
Martyrdom of Bashir Sharab in Khan Younis

(In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. And never think of those who have been killed in the cause of God as dead. Rather, they are alive with their Lord, receiving provision.)

With hearts believing in God's decree and destiny, the Al-Aqqad family mourns the loss of their son-in-law, the martyr Bashir Muhammad Bashir Sharab "Abu Zubaida," husband of the daughter of Professor Muhammad Abdul Rahman Mustafa Al-Aqqad (Abu Halim), and brother-in-law of the martyr Mahmoud Ahmad Muhammad Al-Aqqad "Abu Anas," who was martyred in an Israeli airstrike targeting the Al-Manara neighborhood of Khan Younis on Saturday, December 16, 2023. We ask God Almighty to have mercy on him, forgive him, and grant him a spacious place in Paradise with the prophets and the righteous. May He inspire his family with patience and solace, and we consider him a martyr in God's eyes.

Indeed, we belong to God, and indeed, to Him we will return.

#Official_Al-Aqqad_Family_Website See less — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/18EcKCyp5Y/
The martyrdom of our dear cousin, Nidal Taher Sharab, and his son, Taher Nidal Sharab, who joined their beloved martyrs Khalil Marwan Sharab and Bashir Muhammad Sharab, after being injured along with them in the bombing of their home in the Al-Manara neighborhood.

We belong to God, and to Him we shall return.

O God, forgive them, have mercy on them, and accept them as martyrs.
In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

On the second anniversary of their martyrdom
From December 16, 2023 to December 16, 2025
Two years have passed, and the pain remains, and your memory lives on in our hearts, never to fade.

Today, we stand with reverence and pride before the pure souls that ascended, leaving behind an unforgettable legacy and a wound that will not heal, but with it comes dignity, patience, and acceptance.

We remember with pride and loyalty:
My brother, the martyr Bashir Muhammad Sharab
My brother-in-law, the martyr Nidal Taher Sharab
My brother-in-law, the martyr Mahmoud Ahmad Al-Aqqad
My nephew, the martyr Taher Nidal Sharab
You departed this world, but you dwell in our hearts and memories. You have become a story of patience to be told, and a banner of dignity that will never be lowered. We long for your faces, your voices, your unfulfilled dreams, but we believe that God has chosen you to be in His highest abode, alive and provided for.

O God, accept them among the martyrs, elevate their ranks in Paradise, and grant them Paradise as their final resting place.

Strengthen our hearts and inspire us with patience and solace.

We are truly saddened by their departure, but we are resigned to God's will.

You will remain a pulse that never dies, a memory that never fades.

Peace be upon your pure souls until the day we meet again. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14ZhM1QhbcJ/

December 16, 2023
We will never forget you until we meet again. We will cherish your memory until we meet again. May you rest in peace, and may your memory be blessed. You will forever remain in our hearts. Your memory still fills our hearts. We will never forget you. May God have mercy on the martyrs:

#Martyr/Bashir Muhammad Sharab
#Martyr/Nidal Taher Sharab
#Martyr/Taher Nidal Sharab

With hearts believing in God's decree and destiny, the Al-Aqqad family mourns the loss of their son-in-law, the martyr Bashir Muhammad Bashir Sharab "Abu Zubaida," husband of the daughter of Professor Muhammad Abdul Rahman Mustafa Al-Aqqad (Abu Halim), and brother-in-law of the martyr Mahmoud Ahmad Muhammad Al-Aqqad "Abu Anas," who was martyred in an Israeli airstrike targeting the Al-Manara neighborhood of Khan Younis on Saturday, December 16, 2023. We ask God Almighty to have mercy on him, forgive him, and grant him a spacious place in Paradise with the prophets and the righteous. May He inspire his family with patience and solace, and we consider him a martyr in God's eyes.

Indeed, we belong to God, and indeed, to Him we will return.

#Official_Al-Aqqad_Family_Website See less — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/18EcKCyp5Y/
The martyrdom of our dear cousin, Nidal Taher Sharab, and his son, Taher Nidal Sharab, who joined their beloved martyrs Khalil Marwan Sharab and Bashir Muhammad Sharab, after being injured along with them in the bombing of their home in the Al-Manara neighborhood.

We belong to God, and to Him we shall return.

O God, forgive them, have mercy on them, and accept them as martyrs.
In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

On the second anniversary of their martyrdom
From December 16, 2023 to December 16, 2025
Two years have passed, and the pain remains, and your memory lives on in our hearts, never to fade.

Today, we stand with reverence and pride before the pure souls that ascended, leaving behind an unforgettable legacy and a wound that will not heal, but with it comes dignity, patience, and acceptance.

We remember with pride and loyalty:
My brother, the martyr Bashir Muhammad Sharab
My brother-in-law, the martyr Nidal Taher Sharab
My brother-in-law, the martyr Mahmoud Ahmad Al-Aqqad
My nephew, the martyr Taher Nidal Sharab
You departed this world, but you dwell in our hearts and memories. You have become a story of patience to be told, and a banner of dignity that will never be lowered. We long for your faces, your voices, your unfulfilled dreams, but we believe that God has chosen you to be in His highest abode, alive and provided for.

O God, accept them among the martyrs, elevate their ranks in Paradise, and grant them Paradise as their final resting place.

Strengthen our hearts and inspire us with patience and solace.

We are truly saddened by their departure, but we are resigned to God's will.

You will remain a pulse that never dies, a memory that never fades.

Peace be upon your pure souls until the day we meet again. — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/14ZhM1QhbcJ/

On October 9th 2023, an alleged strike by the Israeli military resulted in the destruction of the old municipality building of Khan Yunis and the surrounding area. At least four civilians were killed in this attack. Through various social media posts, they were identified as Hani Ghassan Qudyah (19 years old), Ahmed Shehdeh Abu Teir (53 years old), Shehda Abu Latifa and Wissam al-Daghma (18 years old). The posts noted that prior to this attack, they were fleeing from the east of the Khan Yunis district, with one source adding that 50 others were injured.

Social media posts about funerals identified the four men referenced above. A Facebook post by Sharif Shurrab is the only post to mention a fifth casualty. Based on a report by Reuters, it is likely that this death is that of a 4 year old girl named Shahib Abu Rokbah — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0045-october-9-2023/
Assessment:
Telegram: Martyr Commander/ Ahmed Shehadeh Abu Teir

(Platoon Commander - Support Unit - Khan Younis Brigade)

Incident Summary:
On 17 October 2023, an explosion occurred at Al-Ahli Arab Hospital in Gaza.
Based on several independent investigations and intelligence assessments, the incident is most consistent with a misfired Palestinian rocket that failed shortly after launch and impacted the hospital grounds, rather than an air-delivered strike. The casualties are therefore classified as Non-Hostile (War-Related) — Internal Fire / Munition Misfire.

Incident Quotes:
At least 11 members of the Hamad family were killed and others were injured in an alleged Israeli airstrike on their home on Al-Zohour Street, in Camp one of Nuseirat Refugee Camp in Deir al-Balah on July 15, 2024.

The deceased family members were identified as: grandfather Misbah Hussein Hamad “Abu Muhammad”, 61-62; grandmother Zuhur Muhammad Misbah Hamad Hamdan “Umm Muhammad”, 60; their sons Abdul Rahim Misbah Hussein Hamad “Abu Ayhem”, 32-35; Jihad Misbah Hussein Hamad, 20; and Khairuddin Misbah Hussein Hamad, 28-30; and their daughter Ala Misbah Hussein Hamad, 39-40. Misbah and Zuhur’s daughter-in-law, Abdul Rahim’s wife Zahra Wael Farag Hamad Mansour, 27, was also killed along with Abdul Rahim and Zahra’s sons Ayham Abdul Rahim Misbah Hamad and Awsam Abdul Rahim Misbah Hamad. While a post from Ahmed Rezeq reported the ages of Ayham and Awsam as six and three respectively, the Ministry of Health lists Ayham and Awsam as both being two years old, twins born on May 20, 2022. Images of the two shared on social media indicate that they were young twins, pictured in matching outfits; Airwars has therefore recorded the MoH ages next to the names of the children below.

Misbah and Zuhur’s other grandchildren, granddaughter Jouri Mahmoud Misbah Hamad, 6-7 (referred to as “Judy Muhammad” in some posts but clarified by her uncle Ahmed, she is the daughter of Mahmoud based on her name), and their grandson Obaida Mahmoud Misbah Hamad, 5-6 (son of their son Mahmoud and sibling of Jouri based on his name) were also killed. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt150724i-july-15-2024/

Incident Quotes:
At least 23 civilians were killed and “a number” injured after an alleged Israeli airstrike on the Al-Riyati family residence near the Qeshta factory in Rafah, after midnight on 7 November, 2023.

68-year-old Musa Youssef Award al-Riyati was killed with his wife, Abla Hussein Mohammed al-Riyati, and his four daughters: his eldest, 44-year-old Mai; 29-year-old Manar; 29-year-old Maryam; and 26-year-old Malak. Maryam’s husband, 36-year-old Ala’a Shehadeh Abu Luli was also killed, as were their children: 7-year-old Ali, 6-year-old Malak, and 5-year-old Jude.

Ala’a’s brother, 30-year-old Mahmoud Shahada Abu Luli was also killed.

Other members of the Al-Riyati family were also killed: 20-year-old Mohammed Nour Mohammed al-Riyati, 19-year-old Mohsen Mohammed al-Riyati, and Mira Abdullah al-Riyati. A further three children from the family were also killed: 8-year-old Salah Mohamed Mohammed al-Riyati, and Kinda and Karam Mohammed Mahmoud al-Riyati, both two-years-old.

Three siblings from the Al-Ajili family were also reported killed: 23-year-old Aya Atallah Musa al-Ajili, as well as 18-year-old Youssef Atallah Musa al-Ajili and 15-year-old Safwat Atallah Musa al-Ajili.

A further three civilians were killed: 44-year-old Adnan Hamdan Abu Arar, 26-year-old Ezz El-Din Zenon and Badr Ahmed Masoud. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0943-november-7-2023/

Incident Quotes:
The first anniversary of the martyrdom of my brother Suleiman Muhammad Abu Hasira, his wife and children 😢 — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1GuxRx7Zdf/
The martyrdom of my cousin Sulaiman Mohammed Abu Hasira, his wife and children 💔 — https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1fxxAYMGaA/
The martyr Dina al-Bawab al-Sak, her husband Suleiman Abu Hasira (a memorizer of the Quran), her son Muhammad (also a memorizer of the Quran), and her two daughters Zeina and Mira were martyred together. They were surrounded by tanks when the Israelis suddenly entered and besieged al-Shifa Hospital on March 20, 2024.

Her sister said they couldn't get out of the building because the tanks were at the door, and they stayed until dawn on March 20. They bombed their building and they were martyred while fasting. My sister had been calling the Red Cross, asking if they could evacuate them and coordinate with the soldiers, but the Red Cross replied that they couldn't guarantee their safety if they left. My sister's last call to the Red Cross was an hour before the bombing, and they gave the same response.

My sister was about seven months pregnant and working as a pharmacist in the internal medicine department at Al-Shifa Hospital. Her eight-year-old son had memorized the entire Quran, and she had held a ceremony for him at his school two weeks before the war.

They are not just numbers.

On the fortieth anniversary of your passing... Your glory preceded your name, and your deeds preceded your words. Incident Quotes:
On October 19th, 2024, the Israeli military allegedly bombed the UNRWA affiliated Asmaa school in the Al-Shati Refugee Camp, west of Gaza City. At least seven people were killed and there were reports of a number of injuries as well. Among those killed were: Hesham Idris Al-Sheikh Mahmoud, Mohammed Walid Shraim, Imad Jabr Abu Asr, Hussein Abdullah Al-Jojo, Youssef Mohammed Shaheen, Mahmoud Abdullah Abu Al-Qura, and Mohamed Sami Abdel Jawad Jouda.

Three of the men, Mohammed Walid Shraim, Hussein Abdullah al-Jojo, and Youssef Mohamed Shaheen appear to have been members of the police force. While armed groups in Gaza are known to contribute personnel to the Gaza police force, as Airwars did not find any connection between these three men and an armed group, they have been considered among the count of those civilians killed. This will be updated should additional information come to light. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt191024l-october-19-2024/

Incident Quotes:
Among those killed were three generations of the al-Baba family. A 59-year-old grandfather Khaled Hassan Wadi al-Baba (Abu Fadi) was killed alongside his wife, Maha Abdel Karim Basal, one of their sons, the 32-year-old lawyer Muhammad Khaled Hassan al-Baba (Abu Anas), and their three daughters, a 34-year old Azhar Khaled Hassan Wadi al-Baba, a 29-year old Aya Khaled Hassan al-Baba, and a 26-year old Bisan Khaled al-Baba.
Khaled’s daughter-in-law, Wafa Abdel Khaleq al-Suwayrki was killed alongside her one-month-old quadruplets – a daughter named Maha Fadi al-Baba and three sons, Khaled Fadi al-Baba, Abdul Khaleq Fadi al-Baba, and Mahmoud Fadi al-Baba. Various sources, including Wafa’s cousin, Mohannad al-Suwaryki and Twitter/X user @Layann1948, among others, reported that Wafa had been trying to have children for 15 years; her story had recently been featured in news media along with pictures. Facebook user Abdel Kareem Al-Tam posted the images of the four dead infants.

Another daughter-in-law of Khaled and Maha, Nesma al-Yaziji, was also killed alongside her infant daughters – Maha Hassan al-Baba, Miral Hassan al-Baba, and Massa Hassan al-Baba. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0342-october-18-2023/

Various sources, including Wafa’s cousin, Mohannad al-Suwaryki and Twitter/X user @Layann1948, among others, reported that Wafa had been trying to have children for 15 years; her story had recently been featured in news media along with pictures. Facebook user Abdel Kareem Al-Tam posted the images of the four dead infants.

Another daughter-in-law of Khaled and Maha, Nesma al-Yaziji, was also killed alongside her infant daughters – Maha Hassan al-Baba, Miral Hassan al-Baba, and Massa Hassan al-Baba. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0342-october-18-2023/

Between the night of Tuesday, October 24, 2023, and the early hours of Wednesday, October 25, 2023, an alleged Israeli airstrike struck the Abu al-Saud family home in al-Shaboura Camp, central Rafah. The bombing killed at least two men, 31-year-old Qasim Ismail Musa Abu al-Saud and 21-year-old Musa Khaled Khalil Abu al-Saud, and up to eight civilians, and left dozens of others injured while also destroying the building.

Reports stated that the two victims, along with “a number” of injured civilians, were taken to Aby Youssef Al-Najjar Hospital. The injured reportedly numbered in the “dozens,” according to the posts by the Palestinian Joint Operations Room’s and news agency Safa’s Twitter/X accounts. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0604-october-24-2023/

Incident Quotes:
Airwars has been able to identify 13 victims by name from eulogies posted by their families, neighbours, and loved ones, including posts by Abu Abdullah, Sahar Dahleez and Diwan Al Zaroub.

64-year old owner of the house Hajj Adel Abdulaziz Hamad Dahleez was killed alongside his 57-year old wife Hiyam Fayed Mohsen Dahleez and her sister, 65-year old Sawsan Fayed Mohsen Dahleez (Aqla).  Four brothers – a 31-year old Ziad Adel A Dahleez, a 33-year old Abdulaziz Adel Dahleez, a 22-year old Bassem Adel Dehliz, and 30-year-old Hamdi Adel Dahleez were also killed.

Ziad’s 4-year old son Adel Ziad Adel Dahleez was also killed. Ziad was reportedly hugging his son when they died. There are also eulogies for Ziad’s wife, but Airwars has not been able to identify her by name. An image of the dead bodies of Ziad and his son Adel where Ziad is seen hugging his son were shared by Algeria News and Quranarab Facebook account.

Among the victims of the deadly attack were also two of Abdulaziz’s small sons –  a 4-year old Adel Abdulaziz Dahleez and a 6-year old Mohamed Abdulaziz Dahleez. A friend of the late Abdulaziz named Faris Al-Amour Abu Muhammad shared some images where Abdulaziz, a young bearded man in a yellow jacket, is seen embracing his two small smiling sons. In other images he appears at the seaside and at a family festival.

Initially one of the relatives called “Lana’s Mother” on Facebook reported that Hamdi Dahleez and his wife were injured, and their names were not on the list of the killed relatives. However, later it was reported by Hamdi’s relative that Hamdi was in fact killed, though there were no further reports about his wife.

Kamal Abu Aaed shared the list of 11 names and added some images of the victims, among them an elderly man, apparently Adel Abdulaziz Hamad Dahleez, and three young men who are apparently his deceased sons. The Zourab family, expressing the condolences to the surviving family members, also shared the same image of Adel.

A 14-year old girl named Afnan Aladdin Ahmed Abu Younis was also among those killed and her father posted a photo of her phone background: a lantern with a Hadith of the Prophet Muhammad written.

Lastly, Aseel Attia Dahleez was also killed as per testimonies of family members and friends. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0474-october-22-2023/

Lastly, Aseel Attia Dahleez was also killed as per testimonies of family members and friends. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0474-october-22-2023/

Airwars has been able to identify 13 victims by name from eulogies posted by their families, neighbours, and loved ones, including posts by Abu Abdullah, Sahar Dahleez and Diwan Al Zaroub.

64-year old owner of the house Hajj Adel Abdulaziz Hamad Dahleez was killed alongside his 57-year old wife Hiyam Fayed Mohsen Dahleez and her sister, 65-year old Sawsan Fayed Mohsen Dahleez (Aqla).  Four brothers – a 31-year old Ziad Adel A Dahleez, a 33-year old Abdulaziz Adel Dahleez, a 22-year old Bassem Adel Dehliz, and 30-year-old Hamdi Adel Dahleez were also killed.

Ziad’s 4-year old son Adel Ziad Adel Dahleez was also killed. Ziad was reportedly hugging his son when they died. There are also eulogies for Ziad’s wife, but Airwars has not been able to identify her by name. An image of the dead bodies of Ziad and his son Adel where Ziad is seen hugging his son were shared by Algeria News and Quranarab Facebook account.

Among the victims of the deadly attack were also two of Abdulaziz’s small sons –  a 4-year old Adel Abdulaziz Dahleez and a 6-year old Mohamed Abdulaziz Dahleez. A friend of the late Abdulaziz named Faris Al-Amour Abu Muhammad shared some images where Abdulaziz, a young bearded man in a yellow jacket, is seen embracing his two small smiling sons. In other images he appears at the seaside and at a family festival.

Initially one of the relatives called “Lana’s Mother” on Facebook reported that Hamdi Dahleez and his wife were injured, and their names were not on the list of the killed relatives. However, later it was reported by Hamdi’s relative that Hamdi was in fact killed, though there were no further reports about his wife.

Kamal Abu Aaed shared the list of 11 names and added some images of the victims, among them an elderly man, apparently Adel Abdulaziz Hamad Dahleez, and three young men who are apparently his deceased sons. The Zourab family, expressing the condolences to the surviving family members, also shared the same image of Adel.

A 14-year old girl named Afnan Aladdin Ahmed Abu Younis was also among those killed and her father posted a photo of her phone background: a lantern with a Hadith of the Prophet Muhammad written.

Lastly, Aseel Attia Dahleez was also killed as per testimonies of family members and friends. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0474-october-22-2023/

Assessment: Telegram: Israeli occupation aircraft targeted the home of the Dahleez family in Rafah Governorate without prior warning or notice, resulting in the martyrdom of Asil Abu Hamida “Dahleez” and a number of family members.

Facebook: The martyr—in our belief—lives more intensely in death than in life.

In his martyrdom, he is a fighter, a field commander, or an inspiring symbol.

But in his martyrdom, he is a giver of life, a legend of values. No place can contain him, and no time can confine him.

Peace be upon you, Omar. Peace be upon you, O martyr.

Transliterated Name: Mohammad Yousef Abd al-Ftah Abu Mthkoar
Arabic Name: محمد يوسف عبد الفتاح ابو مذكور
Incident Summary: At least 5 combatants were killed in this incident, including Platoon commander.
Incident Quotes: The victims of the deadly attack were named as 25-year-old Walid Tawfiq Salameh Abu Suhaiban, 26-year-old Abdel Fattah Youssef Abu Mathkur, 54-year-old Youssef Raed Youssef Abu Mathkur, 36-year-old Muhammad Youssef Abd al-Fattah Abu Mathkur, and 34-year-old Shadi Talal Nadi Al-Arami. A post by Fady Fouad referred to Muhammad Youssef Abu Mathkur, Abdel Fattah Youssef Abu Mathku, and Youssef Raed Abu Mathkour as “mujahids”. However, there was no additional evidence of their participation in militant activities. Until new information comes to light, Airwars has counted their civilian/militant status as “contested”. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0102q-october-10-2023/
Person Sources: Obituary Affiliation Social Post: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1aJ4pDVx9L/
Assessment:
Facebook: The Ascension of Our Beloved Heroes. 💔

The Mujahid, Muhammad Yusuf Abu Madhkur "Abu Shahab"
The Mujahid, Abd al-Fattah Yusuf Abu Madhkur "Aboud"
The Mujahid, Yusuf Raed Abu Madhkur "son of the beloved Raed Abu Yusuf"
May God have mercy on you, forgive you, and accept you, O shining stars...

Incident Summary: At least 1 combatant (Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ)) was killed in this incident, along with 4 relatives.
Incident Quotes:
On October 10th, 2023 at dawn, an alleged Israeli airstrike hit the Al-Qara family’s house in the town of Bani Suhayla in eastern Khan Yunis in the Gaza strip, killing four civilian members of the Al-Qara family, and one militant.

The civilians were identified as Mohamed Ashraf Ismail Al-Qara (23 years old), his sister Waad Ashraf Ismail Al-Qara (18 years old), his other sister Iman Ashraf Ismail Al-Qara and her three week old son Saif Abu Hagras. Another member of the family, eight year old Menna reportedly sustained injuries from the bombing, and was said to be the sole survivor of the strike. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0079-october-10-2023/

Relative Social Media Post: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KCY1oMJaf/

On October 10th, 2023 at dawn, an alleged Israeli airstrike hit the Al-Qara family’s house in the town of Bani Suhayla in eastern Khan Yunis in the Gaza strip, killing four civilian members of the Al-Qara family, and one militant.

The civilians were identified as Mohamed Ashraf Ismail Al-Qara (23 years old), his sister Waad Ashraf Ismail Al-Qara (18 years old), his other sister Iman Ashraf Ismail Al-Qara and her three week old son Saif Abu Hagras. Another member of the family, eight year old Menna reportedly sustained injuries from the bombing, and was said to be the sole survivor of the strike. — https://airwars.org/civilian-casualties/ispt0079-october-10-2023/
